data.title: Trainline PLC Executes Share Buyback Program
data.description:
Trainline Plc (GB:TRN) has released an update.

Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

Trainline PLC has announced the buyback and cancellation of 70,409 of its ordinary shares, with the transaction executed by Morgan Stanley & Co. The share buyback is part of a programme announced in September 2023, following the authorization from the company’s Annual General Meeting. Post-purchase, the total number of ordinary shares in issue stands at 468,659,921.

data.title: GENOVA Inc. Revises 2026 Earnings Forecast Following Strategic Acquisition
data.description:
Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

GENOVA Inc. ( (JP:9341) ) has provided an announcement.

GENOVA Inc. has revised its earnings forecast for the fiscal year 2026 due to a recent acquisition of a portion of ADI.G Co., Ltd.’s business. The revision reflects an increase in net sales but a decrease in profits, attributed to the conservative estimation of goodwill and intangible assets related to the acquisition. This strategic move is expected to impact GENOVA’s financial outlook and stakeholder expectations.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:9341) stock is a Buy with a Yen58.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GENOVA Inc. stock, see the JP:9341 Stock Forecast page.

More about GENOVA Inc.

GENOVA Inc. operates in the financial sector and is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market. The company is involved in mergers and acquisitions, with a focus on restructuring support for other companies.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: Yen12.97B

data.title: Dai Nippon Printing Reports Mixed Financial Results for Q1 2025
data.description:
Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

Dai Nippon Printing Co ( (JP:7912) ) has provided an update.

Dai Nippon Printing Co., Ltd. reported a mixed financial performance for the first quarter of 2025, with a slight increase in net sales by 2.7% compared to the previous year. However, the company experienced a significant decline in net income attributable to parent company shareholders by 28.4%. Despite the challenges, the company showed growth in operating income and ordinary income, indicating operational improvements. The company’s equity ratio slightly decreased, reflecting changes in its financial structure. The announcement highlights the company’s ongoing efforts to adjust its dividend strategy and manage its financial forecasts amidst a challenging economic environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:7912) stock is a Buy with a Yen2890.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dai Nippon Printing Co stock, see the JP:7912 Stock Forecast page.

More about Dai Nippon Printing Co

Dai Nippon Printing Co., Ltd. operates in the printing and information industry, offering a range of products and services including smart communication, life and healthcare, and electronics. The company is focused on leveraging its technological capabilities to serve various market segments.

Average Trading Volume: 1,195,215

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

data.title: Shinagawa Refractories Reports Mixed Financial Results for Q1 2025
data.description:
Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

Shinagawa Refractories Co., Ltd. ( (JP:5351) ) has issued an announcement.

Shinagawa Refractories Co., Ltd. reported its financial results for the three months ended June 30, 2025, showing a 13% increase in sales compared to the previous year. However, the company experienced a decline in operating and ordinary profits by 5.3% and 14.3%, respectively, with net income attributable to owners of the parent dropping by 39.7%. Despite these challenges, the company maintains its dividend forecast for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, and has revised its earnings forecast, projecting a 22.2% increase in sales for the full year.

More about Shinagawa Refractories Co., Ltd.

Shinagawa Refractories Co., Ltd. operates in the refractories industry, specializing in the production of refractory materials used primarily in high-temperature industrial processes. The company is listed on the Tokyo and Sapporo stock exchanges.

data.description:
Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

Focus Systems Corporation ( (JP:4662) ) has issued an update.

Focus Systems Corporation has announced a revision in its dividend forecast for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, increasing the total annual dividend to 54.00 yen per share from the previously forecasted 50.00 yen. This decision follows a significant increase in profits due to successful project acquisitions and extraordinary income from the sale of investment securities, allowing the company to enhance shareholder returns and invest in growth.

More about Focus Systems Corporation

Focus Systems Corporation operates in the technology sector, providing services and products aimed at enhancing growth, profitability, and returns for stakeholders. The company is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market and is focused on capturing high-profit projects and increasing profitability.

data.title: Kanto Denka Kogyo Reports Decline in Q1 FY2025 Profits
data.description:
Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

The latest announcement is out from Kanto Denka Kogyo Co., Ltd. ( (JP:4047) ).

Kanto Denka Kogyo Co., Ltd. reported a decrease in net sales by ¥0.3 billion in the first quarter of fiscal year 2025, primarily due to the absence of technical support fees for battery materials, despite increased sales of specialty gases. The company’s operating profit fell by ¥0.4 billion, attributed to a decline in sales and an increase in inventory valuation losses, while ordinary profit decreased by ¥1.1 billion due to reduced foreign exchange gains.

More about Kanto Denka Kogyo Co., Ltd.

Kanto Denka Kogyo Co., Ltd. operates in the chemical industry, focusing on the production of specialty gases and materials used in semiconductor manufacturing and battery materials.
